column4: A good advertising plan for me would be little to no cost for starters. We're consistently tight on budget and so advertising takes a back seat often. I would want proof that the advertising is working and actually bringing customers in. The perfect scenario would be something to bring people in to redeem something during our slower hours so it's a huge win for our team, it doesn't add to any chaos of redeeming something during a busy rush, we can clearly see people are responding to the ad, and our investment was minimal to begin with.

column4: For this business, a really good marketing plan would be a strong social media presence across all platforms, posting informative information for people relative to chiropractic care and rehab, fun posts, CTA posts, reels, posts, articles. Also a strong google ads presence to stay competitive and see results back. I would also like to have a consistent newsletter that goes out twice a month to our current patient base and to new clients who sign up online. Something that I have also read and am interested in pursuing is AI marketing, soon many people will just ask AI to recommend a service and I want to make sure we are on the cutting edge and get in that space early.

column4: For me, a good advertising plan would just make things clear, where to focus, who I’m targeting, and what kind of ads will actually work. I’d want to know how to spend my budget wisely and what results I should expect. Most importantly, I need a simple way to see what’s working so I can adjust and put more money into the ads that are bringing in sales. If I can clearly see that, I’d feel confident sticking with the plan.
column5: I’d put most of it into Facebook/Instagram and Google Ads to bring in traffic and see what actually converts. I’d also keep a small part for improving creatives, since better content usually makes a big difference. The main goal would be to figure out what’s working quickly and then put more money into the ads that are driving sales.
